title: Incorrect Liquidation Health Check Logic
reason: The extra `shortfall > 0` check runs after `doTransferIn` but before borrow storage is reduced, so it is not validating post-liquidation health. It is a mid-transfer reentrancy defense, not inverted liquidation logic.

title: Flash Loan State Inconsistency Allows Reentrancy
reason: During the callback, `totalBorrows` is temporarily increased but `internalCash` has already been reduced by the same loan amount, preserving core exchange-rate accounting. No concrete exploit path or accounting break was substantiated beyond generic reentrancy speculation.

title: Unprotected gulp() Function Allows Reserve Manipulation
reason: `gulp()` only converts excess underlying already sitting in the contract into reserves. Anyone can already donate tokens to the market, and calling `gulp()` does not create attacker profit, fund loss, or meaningful protocol harm.

title: Missing Access Control on Collateral Cap Setting
reason: `_setCollateralCap` explicitly requires `msg.sender == admin`. This is a style inconsistency at most, not an access-control vulnerability.

title: Unused isNative Parameter in doTransferIn/doTransferOut
reason: Unused parameters are code smell only and do not create a realistic security impact here.

title: Liquidation liveness depends on an extra post-transfer liquidity check inside a callback-capable repayment path
reason: This scenario is highly speculative and mainly relies on callback behavior during `transferFrom` to change the borrower's cross-market liquidity mid-transaction. The most direct callback control is with the liquidator/payer, who can already sabotage their own liquidation, so a realistic third-party protocol-level exploit was not established.

title: Flashloan callback magic value is non-standard and rejects standard ERC-3156 receivers
reason: The mismatch is a compatibility/integration issue, but by itself it does not show realistic fund loss, theft, insolvency, or durable denial of service for the protocol. It is not sufficiently reportable as a security finding on this record.
